rationale: In the literature positioning, Manchanda et al. (2006) is not just listed as background; it is part of a defined prior stream that the new model explicitly moves beyond from single-channel to multichannel analysis. Later, the citing paper names this study directly when discussing results and claims consistency with its empirical findings on display advertising and purchase behavior. This gives the citation a clear framing and interpretive role, even though the cited method is not directly reused.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Manchanda et al. (2006) as prior evidence that banner/display advertising can affect online purchasing, then extends that line by modeling cross-channel carryover and spillover effects for attribution. It also uses the cited paper's specific display-ad findings to interpret its own purchase-stage results.
